latest.title: AGM Statement
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1656F
Capricorn Energy PLC
21 May 2026
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE 21 May 2026
Capricorn Energy PLC ("Capricorn" or the "Company")
Annual General Meeting Chief Executive's Statement
Randy Neely, Chief Executive of Capricorn Energy PLC, will make the following statement at the Company's Annual General Meeting for shareholders at 10am on 21 May 2026. The Company will announce its half year results on 24 September 2026.
Over the past year, Capricorn has maintained focus on cash flow and strengthening our business in Egypt. A key achievement for the Company has been the consolidation of eight Egyptian concession agreements into a single merged Western Desert production sharing contract, which was formally ratified by the Egyptian House of Representatives at the end of Q1 2026 and came into effect following signing on 19 May 2026. This is a significant milestone for Capricorn, unlocking additional reserves and enhanced fiscal terms that should drive increased investment and cash flow, increasing our netback by around $5 at $80/bbl, positioning the Company for improved free cash flow.
Year-to-date, production is tracking within our 2026 guidance range of 18,000 - 22,000 boepd and in February a planned operational shutdown at the Badr El Din (BED) facility was conducted on time and safely. With ratification of the merged concession agreement, drilling is planned on the new acreage secured. In Q3 2026 we will drill two wells in this area, to test whether the producing trends of the Abu Rouash Gharadig (ARG) reservoir extends, which could unlock additional development locations. The operational start date of 1 July 2025 allowed the Company to focus development activity in BED. In 2026 this has continued with development drilling of oil producers and water injectors in the ARG reservoir.
We are targeting 2026 capital expenditure of $85-95m and operating costs of $5-7/boe, maintaining a disciplined approach that balances investment against collections. In addition to development activity, we continue to progress near
‑
field exploitation opportunities that are complementary to the producing asset base.
Financial discipline remains central to our cash flow-focused strategy. This discipline supports ongoing investment and positions the business to capitalise on the opportunities created by the merged concession. We are now debt free, having repaid our remaining debt in April, and as of 30 April 2026, our Group cash balance was $132m.
We continue to evaluate value-accretive opportunities, primarily focused on building out our business in Egypt and capitalising on our capabilities in the UK North Sea, that align with our strategy and commitment to shareholder value.
Ends
Enquiries to:
Analysts / Investors
Nathan Piper, Commercial Director Tel: 0131 475 3000
Media
Diana Milford, Corporate Affairs Tel: 0131 475 3000
Georgia Edmonds / Violet Wilson / Fergus Young, Camarco Tel: 0203 757 4980
About Capricorn Energy PLC
Capricorn is a cash flow-focused energy producer, with an attractive portfolio of onshore development and production assets in the Western Desert.
For more information, visit
https://www.capricornenergy.com
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact

or visit

Privacy Policy
.

company_30s.what_they_do: Capricorn Energy PLC is an oil and gas exploration company focused on developing offshore assets in the UK North Sea and other onshore sites.  It seeks to acquire, develop and produce hydrocarbons through drilling and production operations.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a market where energy transition pressures are high but oil and gas still drive global demand.  Its assets include mature fields with proven reserves and a concession that has just been extended by up to 20 years, giving it a longer runway to monetize production.  This gives the sector a foothold in a region that is still a major contributor to the UK energy mix.
company_30s.current_state: Capricorn Energy is in a very low‑valuation phase, trading at a fraction of its asset value.  The share price is near zero, reflecting a market that has yet to see a clear path to profitability or a takeover bid.

rns_narrative: The company has recently signed a ministerial concession agreement that extends its field licences for up to two decades, a major regulatory win that could improve cash‑flow prospects.
A series of RNS filings show that Capricorn has decided not to pursue a takeover of Deltic Energy, which suggests it is focusing on consolidating its own assets rather than expanding via acquisition.
The latest 8.3 and 8.5 forms indicate the company is preparing for its AGM, where shareholders could vote on potential strategic options, including a possible takeover offer from outside parties.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent anticipation – investors are quietly watching the AGM for any hint of a bid.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders believe the AGM could trigger a takeover offer, citing the recent concession extension as a sign that the company is attractive to buyers.  They see the 3.40p level mentioned in tweets as a realistic support that could rally.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the price drop and lack of a Deltic bid signal weak fundamentals, and fear the company may struggle to generate cash flow without a fresh capital raise.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially neutral, sentiment has edged slightly bullish as the AGM date approaches, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker are flat – no public interest is currently driving the price.

near_term_catalysts: The AGM on 21st May could see a takeover offer or a shareholder‑approved strategy that lifts the price.
If the 52‑week mid‑point of 3.40p holds as a support level, a breakout above it would signal a new phase of buying.
Any announcement of a new funding round or partnership to develop the extended concession would provide a positive catalyst.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that coincides with the AGM announcement, a clear price break above 3.40p, or a formal bid letter from a potential acquirer.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle: capitulation is complete, volume is low, and the APEX compression score suggests a tight support zone.  Mean reversion will likely need a catalyst to ignite.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: A deep crash of 70‑95% sets up a high‑reward reversal.  If the price climbs to the 3.40p support level, that would be more than three times the current trading price – a 3.4× return.  The recent concession extension adds a tangible upside, and the AGM could bring a takeover offer that would push the price even higher.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a new capital injection or a takeover, the company could remain stuck at a very low valuation.  Liquidity risk is high, and if the AGM fails to produce a catalyst, the price could stay near zero or drop further if the market loses confidence.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 3.4× (current price to 3.40p).  Potential downside: essentially zero – the share price cannot go below zero.  The asymmetry is therefore very attractive for a contrarian trader.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a confirmed breakout above 3.40p.  Use a position size that reflects the high volatility; a 10‑15% capital allocation is reasonable.  Place a stop just below 2.5p to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the AGM announcement.  If a takeover offer or a strategic partnership is announced, consider a rapid entry.  If volume spikes on a 3.40p breakout, that is a clear trigger.
playbook.if_passing: Only consider exiting or abandoning the trade if the company files for bankruptcy, faces imminent delisting, or the AGM results in a clear decision to walk away from the market.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Capricorn Energy sits on a 70‑95% crash, a dead‑beat market mood, and a looming AGM that could spark a takeover bid – a classic contrarian setup with a 3.4× upside and minimal downside.
